description: One who carries messages between people or places; a bearer of communication. 'Toloi' names who bridges distance—carrying words across space.
sound_symbolism: The word carries with purpose. 'To' touches with contact made, message received. 'Lo' opens with delivered content, meaning arriving. Then 'i' rises to understanding received.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'toloi lo phelui kolua.' (The messenger carries words.) Related to 'piru' (trader) as word-carrier to goods-carrier.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Messenger as bridge—enabling communication across distance and difference.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Messenger as connector—linking distant people through carried words.
